title: Element Manifest
description: Structured output of the IFC Extraction Service (F-04): one manifest per (IFC file, discipline), cached by SHA256. Consumed by the BOX voxelization engine (stage-4-ifc.ts -> manifestElementToBoxElement -> voxel-rasterizer.service.ts). Canonical shape transcribed from the live TypeScript source (@ectropy/schemas/types/ifc-extraction.types.ts + bundle.types.ts, both read directly this session) — this schema did not exist in the registry before, so nothing enforced the TS interface and the Python extraction service (extract-speckle-elements.py) agreed on shape. That gap is structurally how the 2026-07-01 Five Whys bbox-corruption defect went uncaught: compute_bbox() could silently emit a wrong shape and nothing would fail. This schema is a conformance contract, not a plausibility check — see the 'Explicitly out of scope' note on bbox below.

ifcDiscipline.description: Which discipline's IFC file this manifest was extracted from. One manifest per (project, discipline) pair — see bundle.manifest_cache[discipline] in stage-4-ifc.ts.

systemType.description: Building system classification, assigned by extract-speckle-elements.py's classify_system(). Real IFC-entity mapping as of this schema's authoring (classification logic itself stays in code — this is documentation, not an enforced constraint on ifc_type): ARCH <- IfcWall(StandardCase), IfcSlab, IfcRoof, IfcDoor, IfcWindow, IfcStair(Flight), IfcRailing, IfcCovering, IfcCurtainWall, IfcPlate, IfcMember, IfcFurniture, IfcBuildingElementProxy (+ prefix fallback IfcWall*/IfcSlab*/IfcRoof*); STRUCT <- IfcBeam, IfcColumn, IfcFoundation, IfcPile, IfcFooting, IfcReinforcingBar, IfcReinforcingMesh (+ prefix fallback IfcBeam*/IfcColumn*/IfcReinforc*); HVAC <- IfcDuctSegment/Fitting, IfcAirTerminal(Box), IfcUnitaryEquipment, IfcCoil, IfcFan, IfcFilter, IfcDamper, IfcFlowController (+ prefix fallback IfcDuct*/IfcAir*); PLUMB <- IfcPipeSegment/Fitting, IfcValve, IfcSanitaryTerminal, IfcFireSuppression, IfcFlowTerminal (+ prefix fallback IfcPipe*/IfcSanitary*); ELEC <- IfcCableCarrierSegment, IfcCableSegment, IfcElectricAppliance, IfcLightFixture, IfcElectricDistributionBoard, IfcProtectiveDevice, IfcJunctionBox (+ prefix fallback IfcCable*/IfcLight*); UNKNOWN <- no ifc_type, or no match anywhere above. CIVIL is part of the type contract (bundle.types.ts SystemType union) but classify_system() has no path that currently produces it — included here because the schema documents the contract, not just the classifier's present reach.

bbox3D.description: World-space bounding box in metres (Speckle coordinate system). Explicitly out of scope for this schema: any plausibility constraint on the values (e.g. max_z - min_z under some threshold). A shape-validation schema cannot distinguish a genuinely tall element from a corrupted one — that class of check belongs in a runtime sanity gate, not here. This schema validates that the six fields exist and are numbers; it does not and should not judge whether the numbers are believable.

storeyRecord.description: One building storey, as extracted from IfcBuildingStorey. storeys[] is ordered ascending by elevation (contract guarantee, not enforced by this schema — array ordering is not expressible in Draft-07 as a constraint on relative element values).

IFCElement.description: One extracted IFC element. Contract guarantees (per ifc-extraction.interface.ts, enforced by the extraction service, not re-enforced here beyond basic shape): guid non-empty and unique within a manifest; system never null; level never null or empty; bbox min strictly less than max on all axes.

guid.description: IFC GlobalId — permanent identifier, unique within the building model (and within this manifest's elements[]).

ifc_type.description: IFC entity type string, e.g. 'IfcWall', 'IfcDuctSegment'. Free string, not an enum — the IFC entity vocabulary is large and versioned by the IFC schema itself, not by this registry.

level.description: Storey name from IfcBuildingStorey, e.g. 'Level 1', 'Roof'. Never empty per the extraction service's contract.

level_elevation.description: Storey elevation in metres above datum.

attributes.description: Additional IFC attributes (material, fire rating, grid reference, etc.). Free-form per the existing contract — this manifest layer does not constrain attribute shape.

ifc_filename.description: Source IFC file name this manifest was extracted from.

storeys.description: Building storeys, ordered ascending by elevation (contract guarantee — see storeyRecord description).

elements.description: The extracted elements themselves. element_count, when present, is expected to equal elements.length (contract guarantee, not enforced here — Draft-07 cannot express 'this integer equals that array's length' as a constraint).
